title: Flink Windowing & Watermarks Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Tumbling, Sliding, and Session windows, along with handling late data.

questions.question: Which Flink window type is dynamic in size and closes only after a defined period of inactivity?

options.text: Tumbling Windows
Sliding Windows
Session Windows
Global Windows

questions.question: What happens in a Flink Tumbling Window configuration where the size is 1 hour?

options.text: Windows are overlapping.
Each event belongs to exactly one non-overlapping window.
Each event belongs to multiple windows.
Windows expand dynamically.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Watermark in Flink streaming?

options.text: To filter out duplicate events.
To promise Flink that no further events with an older timestamp will arrive, allowing the window to close.
To force a snapshot (Checkpoint) of the internal RocksDB state.
To synchronize data between JobManagers and TaskManagers.

questions.question: If a Watermark threshold is reached and a window computes and outputs its result, what does Flink do if it receives a 'Allowed Lateness' configuration?

options.text: It throws an Error and forces a JobManager restart.
It stores the late data in a Zookeeper queue.
It fires the window logic again, emitting an updated result for that window.
It completely ignores the late data.

questions.question: What is a 'Side Output' in Flink windowing logic used for?

options.text: Replicating data for High Availability.
Separating metrics and telemetry into a different Kafka topic.
Routing events that are later than the Allowed Lateness into a dead-letter stream rather than dropping them.
Splitting a single stream into batch files on S3.
